---
title: "zzcollab Architecture Diagrams"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
---

*2026-05-31 18:52 PDT*

This document collects standard software-architecture views of the zzcollab
system as Mermaid diagrams. The set follows the C4 model (context, container,
component) supplemented by a module-dependency view, two interaction
sequences, a data-flow view of the template-substitution pipeline, a runtime
deployment view, and a project-lifecycle state machine.

The diagrams are written in Mermaid fenced blocks. They render natively when
the file is viewed on GitHub and when the document is rendered with Quarto
(`quarto render`). Under plain `rmarkdown::render()` to `html_document`,
Mermaid blocks require the `DiagrammeR` engine or a Mermaid header include;
the source blocks below remain the canonical, version-controlled definition
regardless of renderer.

The views reflect the source after the 2026-05 simplification: modules are
sourced unconditionally in dependency order (no `require_module` system),
`doctor.sh` is executed as a standalone script, dependency validation is
delegated to the `zzrenvcheck` R package, and there are three profiles
(`minimal`, `analysis`, `rstudio`).

## 1. System context (C4 level 1)

The single highest-level view: who uses zzcollab and which external systems
it integrates with. zzcollab is a generator and orchestrator; it owns no
running service of its own.

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    user["Researcher"]
    subgraph zzc["zzcollab framework"]
        sysnode["zzc CLI + R interface package"]
    end
    docker["Docker Engine"]
    registry["Container registry<br/>(Docker Hub / GHCR)"]
    ppm["Posit Package Manager"]
    gh["GitHub + GitHub Actions"]
    zrc["zzrenvcheck (R package)"]
    user -->|"runs zzc commands"| sysnode
    sysnode -->|"generate / build / run images"| docker
    docker -->|"pull base, push team images"| registry
    docker -->|"install pre-built R binaries"| ppm
    sysnode -->|"create repo, install CI workflows"| gh
    sysnode -->|"validate deps inside the container"| zrc
```

## 2. Containers (C4 level 2)

The deployable and runnable parts of the system and the data stores they
share. The R package is a thin wrapper that shells out to the bash CLI; the
configuration store and template library are the CLI's inputs, and the
generated compendium plus its Docker image are its outputs.

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    user["Researcher"]
    rpkg["R package 'zzcollab'<br/>thin wrapper via safe_system()"]
    cli["zzcollab.sh CLI (bash)"]
    cfg[("Config store<br/>~/.zzcollab/config.yaml<br/>./zzcollab.yaml<br/>templates/bundles.yaml")]
    tmpl[("Template library<br/>templates/")]
    proj[("Generated compendium<br/>DESCRIPTION, renv.lock, .Rprofile,<br/>Dockerfile, Makefile, R/, analysis/")]
    img["Project Docker image"]
    user --> rpkg
    user --> cli
    rpkg -->|"shells out"| cli
    cli -->|"read / write (yq)"| cfg
    cli -->|"render (envsubst)"| tmpl
    cli -->|"scaffold"| proj
    cli -->|"build with cache"| img
```

## 3. Components (C4 level 3) and load order

The internal decomposition of the CLI. `zzcollab.sh` sources the libraries
and modules unconditionally at startup in dependency order
(`constants` then `core` then `templates`, then every module), then
dispatches subcommands. `doctor.sh` is the one exception: it is executed as a
standalone script, not sourced.

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    entry["zzcollab.sh<br/>entry, dispatch, main()"]
    subgraph libg["lib/ (sourced first, in order)"]
        constants["constants.sh<br/>defaults, versions"]
        core["core.sh<br/>logging, safe_cp, guards"]
        templates["templates.sh<br/>substitute_variables, render"]
    end
    subgraph modg["modules/ (sourced after lib)"]
        clim["cli.sh<br/>validators, flag defaults"]
        configm["config.sh<br/>yq load / get / set"]
        profilesm["profiles.sh<br/>bundles.yaml lookups"]
        projectm["project.sh<br/>scaffold compendium"]
        dockerm["docker.sh<br/>Dockerfile gen + build cache"]
        githubm["github.sh<br/>repo + workflows"]
        helpm["help.sh"]
    end
    doctor["doctor.sh<br/>standalone executable"]
    entry --> constants
    constants --> core
    core --> templates
    entry --> clim
    entry --> configm
    entry --> profilesm
    entry --> projectm
    entry --> dockerm
    entry --> githubm
    entry --> helpm
    projectm -->|"install_template"| templates
    dockerm -->|"get_profile_base_image"| profilesm
    configm --> core
    entry -. "bash doctor.sh (executed, not sourced)" .-> doctor
```

## 4. Interaction: generating a project (`zzc analysis`)

The core use case, as a sequence. Configuration is loaded once (memoized),
the compendium is scaffolded with variable substitution, the Dockerfile is
generated from a pinned base image, and the build consults a
content-addressable cache keyed on a hash of the Dockerfile plus `renv.lock`.

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    actor U as Researcher
    participant CLI as zzcollab.sh
    participant CFG as config.sh
    participant PRJ as project.sh
    participant TPL as templates.sh
    participant DOC as docker.sh
    participant DK as Docker
    U->>CLI: zzc analysis
    CLI->>CFG: load_config (single yq pass, memoized)
    CLI->>PRJ: setup_project
    PRJ->>TPL: install_template (DESCRIPTION, Makefile, .Rprofile, ...)
    TPL->>TPL: substitute_variables (envsubst allowlist)
    CLI->>DOC: generate_dockerfile
    DOC->>DOC: extract_r_version / extract_r_packages / derive_system_deps
    DOC->>DOC: generate_dockerfile_inline (heredoc, pinned base:R_VERSION)
    CLI->>DOC: build_docker_image
    DOC->>DOC: compute_dockerfile_hash, find_cached_image
    alt cache hit
        DOC->>DK: docker tag cached-image project:latest
    else cache miss
        DOC->>DK: docker build --label dockerfile.hash
    end
    DK-->>U: project image ready
```

## 5. Interaction: the R interface

The R package functions validate and quote their inputs, then delegate to the
bash CLI through `safe_system()`. No business logic is duplicated in R; the
package is an ergonomic front door.

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    actor U as Researcher
    participant R as R function (e.g. init_project)
    participant SS as safe_system()
    participant CLI as zzcollab.sh
    U->>R: init_project(team, project, profile)
    R->>R: validate args, resolve_team_project()
    R->>SS: safe_system("zzcollab ...", shell-quoted args)
    SS->>CLI: system() shell-out
    CLI-->>SS: exit status / captured stdout
    SS-->>R: result
    R-->>U: TRUE / FALSE
```

## 6. Data flow: the substitution pipeline

The heart of zzcollab is a substitution pipeline that turns configuration
into the generated artifacts. `load_config` flattens the YAML in a single
`yq` pass into `CONFIG_*` variables; `substitute_variables` fills the
allowlisted placeholders in the text templates; the Dockerfile and
`renv.lock` are generated programmatically rather than templated.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    args["CLI flags"]
    ucfg[("~/.zzcollab/config.yaml")]
    pcfg[("./zzcollab.yaml")]
    bundles[("templates/bundles.yaml")]
    load["load_config<br/>single yq pass, _CONFIG_MAP"]
    cvars["CONFIG_* variables"]
    subst["substitute_variables<br/>envsubst allowlist"]
    desc["DESCRIPTION"]
    mk["Makefile"]
    rprof[".Rprofile"]
    renvlock["renv.lock<br/>create_renv_lock_minimal"]
    dfile["Dockerfile<br/>generate_dockerfile_inline"]
    ucfg --> load
    pcfg --> load
    load --> cvars
    args --> cvars
    cvars --> subst
    subst --> desc
    subst --> mk
    subst --> rprof
    bundles -->|"get_profile_base_image"| dfile
    cvars -->|"R_VERSION, BASE_IMAGE"| dfile
    cvars -->|"R version"| renvlock
```

## 7. Runtime deployment

The runtime topology of a generated project. The container is built from a
version-pinned base image; the project directory and a shared host-level renv
cache are bind-mounted at run time, so the container itself stays stateless
and reproducible.

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    subgraph host["Host machine"]
        projdir[("project/ working dir")]
        cache[("~/.cache/R/renv<br/>shared host cache")]
        subgraph cont["Docker container (project image)"]
            ruser["USER analyst<br/>WORKDIR /home/analyst/project"]
        end
    end
    registry["Docker Hub / GHCR"]
    ppm["Posit Package Manager (noble)"]
    projdir -. "-v bind mount" .-> ruser
    cache -. "-v bind mount" .-> ruser
    cont -->|"FROM base:R_VERSION (pinned)"| registry
    cont -->|"renv::restore() binaries"| ppm
```

## 8. Project lifecycle (state)

The states a compendium passes through. The individual subcommands advance it
one step at a time; the `analysis` quickstart performs the whole chain.
`doctor.sh` inspects a built workspace to confirm the Five Pillars are present
and their template version stamps are current.

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Empty
    Empty --> Scaffolded: zzc init
    Scaffolded --> RenvLocked: zzc renv
    RenvLocked --> Dockerized: zzc docker
    Dockerized --> Built: make docker-build
    Scaffolded --> Built: zzc analysis (init + renv + docker + build)
    Built --> [*]
    note right of Built
        doctor.sh verifies the Five Pillars
        and template version stamps
    end note
```

## Notes on scope

These eight views are deliberately a small, standard set rather than the full
UML catalogue. The C4 levels (sections 1 to 3) carry the structural story; the
sequences (4 and 5) and the data-flow view (6) carry the behavioural story;
the deployment (7) and state (8) views carry the runtime and lifecycle story.
A class diagram is omitted because the bash codebase is procedural, and a
code-level (C4 level 4) view is omitted as unnecessary detail for an
architecture document.
